evals.prompt: Design the database schema for a multi-tenant project management SaaS. Entities: Organization (name, plan), User (email, name, role per org), Project (name, description, status), Task (title, description, priority, status, assignee, due date), Comment (body, author). Users can belong to multiple orgs. Tasks belong to a project and have an assignee. Use PostgreSQL.
evals.expected_output: SQL CREATE TABLE statements for all entities with proper types, constraints, indexes, foreign keys, join table for user-org, and migration strategy

assertions.name: all_entities_created
assertions.description: CREATE TABLE for organizations, users, projects, tasks, comments, and user_organizations join table

assertions.name: uuid_primary_keys
assertions.description: All tables use UUID primary keys, not serial/integer

assertions.name: foreign_key_indexes
assertions.description: Every foreign key column has a corresponding index

assertions.name: not_null_constraints
assertions.description: Required fields (name, email, title) have NOT NULL constraints

assertions.name: timestamps_on_all
assertions.description: created_at and updated_at on every table

assertions.name: enum_check_constraints
assertions.description: Status and priority fields use CHECK constraints or enum types

evals.prompt: I need a database schema for an e-commerce product catalog. Products have name, description, price, SKU, and can belong to multiple categories. Products have variants (size, color) each with their own price and stock quantity. Products also have images (url, alt text, sort order). Design for PostgreSQL with Prisma ORM.
evals.expected_output: Prisma schema with Product, Category, ProductVariant, ProductImage models with proper relations and indexes

assertions.name: many_to_many_categories
assertions.description: Product-Category uses a join table or implicit M:N relation

assertions.name: variant_model
assertions.description: ProductVariant model with size, color, price override, and stock quantity

assertions.name: image_sort_order
assertions.description: ProductImage has a sortOrder/position field for ordering

assertions.name: sku_unique
assertions.description: SKU field has a unique constraint

assertions.name: price_type_correct
assertions.description: Price uses Decimal/numeric type, not float

evals.prompt: Design a schema for a chat application. Users can create conversations (1-on-1 or group). Messages belong to a conversation and have a sender. Messages can have attachments (file URL, type, size). Users have a read cursor per conversation (last message they've seen). Need to support querying unread count efficiently.
evals.expected_output: Schema with users, conversations, conversation_members, messages, attachments, read_cursors with indexes optimized for unread count queries

assertions.name: conversation_members_join
assertions.description: Join table for users in conversations (supports both 1-on-1 and group)

assertions.name: read_cursor_model
assertions.description: Read cursor tracks last_read_message_id or last_read_at per user per conversation

assertions.name: message_indexes
assertions.description: Index on (conversation_id, created_at) for efficient message listing

assertions.name: unread_query_support
assertions.description: Schema design enables efficient unread count query (cursor vs message timestamps)

assertions.name: attachment_model
assertions.description: Separate attachment table with file metadata (url, type, size)

evals.prompt: We need to add an audit logging system to our existing PostgreSQL database. Every create, update, and delete on the users, orders, and products tables should be logged with: who made the change, what changed (before/after values), when, and the IP address. Design the audit table and explain how to capture changes.
evals.expected_output: Audit log table with JSONB for before/after values, trigger-based or application-level capture strategy, indexes for querying by entity and user

assertions.name: audit_table_design
assertions.description: Audit table with entity_type, entity_id, action (create/update/delete), user_id, changes, timestamp, ip_address

assertions.name: before_after_values
assertions.description: Changes stored as JSONB with old and new values

assertions.name: capture_strategy
assertions.description: Explains trigger-based and/or application-level approach for capturing changes

assertions.name: query_indexes
assertions.description: Indexes on entity_type+entity_id and user_id+created_at for efficient querying

assertions.name: covers_all_three_tables
assertions.description: Audit applies to users, orders, and products tables

evals.prompt: I'm migrating from MongoDB to PostgreSQL. Our current MongoDB collections: users (with nested address object and tags array), products (with nested specs object and reviews array), and orders (with nested items array containing product references and quantities). Design the PostgreSQL schema that properly normalizes this data.
evals.expected_output: Normalized PostgreSQL schema replacing nested objects with related tables, arrays with join tables, preserving all data with proper foreign keys

assertions.name: normalizes_nested_objects
assertions.description: Nested address becomes a separate addresses table or columns on users

assertions.name: normalizes_arrays
assertions.description: Tags array becomes a tags table with user_tags join table

assertions.name: order_items_table
assertions.description: Nested items array becomes an order_items table with product_id and quantity

assertions.name: reviews_table
assertions.description: Nested reviews become a separate reviews table with product_id foreign key

assertions.name: migration_strategy
assertions.description: Describes data migration approach from MongoDB documents to normalized tables
